free_text: Relative Cross Sections for Nuclear Reactions
Induced by High Energy Neutrons in Light Elements
Neutron beam was produced by the action of
190-Mev deuterons on a 0.5 in. beryllium target.
Neutron beam emerged through an aluminum window in
the wall of the cyclotron tank with flux density of
the order 10E+6 to 10E+7 n/cm2/sec.
Activities of reaction products were
determined by decay curve analysis
